prompt:
Scene:
A square scene where miniature scale is believable through lens behavior, surface detail, and carefully staged environmental cues. Build the frame around a dense arrangement of tiny goods or activity zones. Keep the lighting, camera distance, and spatial organization tuned for a high-signal research image rather than a casual snapshot. The image belongs to the Tilt-Shift Miniature World theme and should feel immediately legible as a prompt atlas theme for tilt-shift miniature world, testing photorealism, physics-materials, style-transfer with gpt image 2..

Subject:
Center the study on a dense arrangement of tiny goods or activity zones. Translate the theme into a concrete visual statement instead of an abstract mood board: the viewer should understand what kind of image this is, what is being tested, and why the composition is difficult. Use the slug idea of tilt shift miniature world as a literal guide for subject matter, materials, and staging. If helpful, borrow the broad visual cues suggested by tilt-shift, but keep the result original and specific to this study.

Important details:
The charm should come from credible micro-world logic: tiny infrastructure, scaled weathering, selective focus, and a clear sense of inhabited smallness. Preserve a disciplined square composition, clear focal hierarchy, and enough secondary information to make the image feel complete rather than underspecified. The image should look polished, novel, and exhibition-ready, with topic-specific credibility coming from the way surfaces, structure, and visual language reinforce Tilt-Shift Miniature World. Avoid generic filler elements that do not serve the main concept.

Use case:
Tilt-Shift Miniature World single study 6/8, testing photorealism, physics-materials, style-transfer through the market table variation.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no giant human hands, no obvious toy-store packaging, no random oversized props, no readable text, no flat diorama cuteness without realism, no muddy depth cues.
